bounded_question: How is carried weight represented in the source, and which values can be parsed reproducibly?

provenance_limitation.finding: The source uses stones-and-pounds notation even for jurisdictions that ordinarily publish carried weight in kilograms.
provenance_limitation.consequence: Source-implied kilograms are deterministic SI conversions of the stored source expression, not independently verified original official metric declarations.
provenance_limitation.analytical_use: The converted kilograms are suitable as a consistent analytical variable when their provenance label is retained.

decisions: Parse canonical stones-and-pounds notation only when the pounds component is between 0 and 13.
Retain parsed stones and parsed pounds separately.
Use source_implied_total_pounds for the exact interpretation of the stored expression.
Use source_implied_kilograms only as a literal SI conversion of the stored expression.
Do not treat reconverted kilograms as independently verified original official metric declarations.
Keep contextual race anomalies separate from weight parse validity.
Allow unseen future values to parse only when they use the same unambiguous canonical notation.
Return unsupported future values as unresolved rather than trimming, normalising or guessing.
Retain official_weight_verified as false until authoritative runner-level enrichment exists.
Do not reopen race-distance analysis or design the final target schema in this notebook.
